CVE-2021-31879.title: GNU Wget through 1.21.1 does not omit the Authorization header up ...
CVE-2021-31879.description: GNU Wget through 1.21.1 does not omit the Authorization header upon a redirect to a different origin, a related issue to CVE-2018-1000007.
CVE-2021-31879.notes: The upstream maintainers of GNU Wget have acknowledged the behavior described in CVE-2021-31879 but do not consider it a vulnerability. Their position is that when users manually add an Authorization header using --header, they are deliberately bypassing the built-in authentication mechanism (--user/--password) and opting into full control over the headers being sent. In this case, Wget treats such users as “power users” who are responsible for understanding the implications of their choices — including the forwarding of sensitive headers across redirects. Since Wget is operating exactly as instructed (sending the header on all requests), and there is no violation of protocol specifications (e.g., RFCs), the maintainers consider this expected behavior rather than a security flaw. Instead of changing the implementation, they suggest documenting the risks of using manual headers during redirections. As such, no fix is planned upstream.
